Module 3: Segmentation & Confirmation

©2017





Module 3: Segmentation & Confirmation

Direct Response 2.0 - Questions to Explore:

Additional Questions:

Themeplates

Themeplate 1: Exclusively Empowered Call Out (MVF1)

THEMEPLATE:

EXAMPLE

Themeplate 2 - “The Bieber”

THEMEPLATE:

EXAMPLE

Themeplate 3 - “The Way-Too-Early Ultimatum”

THEMEPLATE

EXAMPLE:

Themeplate 4 - The “What this says about you” Confirmation (MVF2)

EXAMPLE:

Themeplate 5 - The “What This Says About You” Webinar Confirmation

THEMEPLATE:

EXAMPLE:

Segmentation & Confirmation Checklist



Direct Response 2.0 - Questions to Explore: 



What skills, characteristics, experiences, circumstances, achievements or points of pride, exclusively empower your prospect to achieve the end result and/or embark on this journey? 



(ie. already has a website generating 2K visitors/month, already speaks BASIC French, has only just begun losing their hair, etc.) 





What missteps, regrets, sunk costs, or other sources of disillusionment might they need to forgive themselves for (or be asked for forgiveness for) BEFORE being able to get excited about the outcome again? 





How can any of those previous failures be re-framed as necessary prerequisites for where they are now? 



Additional Questions: 



      4. In what ways, can you add “meaning” to “the click”? 

What does it say about them? 

Their commitment level? 

What they REALLY want? 

What they’ll no longer stand for? 





      5. What’s the “moment of highest tension”? (see exercise in Module 2)



Themeplates

Themeplate 1: Exclusively Empowered Call Out (MVF1) 



About: Use this themeplate to invite prospects into the journey, and more importantly, get them excited about it through a sense of exclusive empowerment. 





THEMEPLATE: 



[CTA1]

[Big outcome your webinar/series/launch is promising]

[The Moment of Highest Tension] 

[CTA2] 

[Build up Interest around the “event”] 

[Exclusive Empowered Close] 



EXAMPLE



Subject:

should you be here?



Body:

Hey!



I just wanted to reach out real quick.



Tomorrow we’ll be kicking off our brand new Profit & Lunch Workshop.  



You’re gonna learn all about how to quickly write those crazy good, high-converting emails that can make or break your funnel or course launch.



Y’know…



… the type of emails that look harmless enough on a funnel map, until you realize those harrowing little bastards actually need tender loving care to grow and mature into automated, revenue-generating badasses. 



… the types of emails that you cause you to drunk-post messages on FB at 3AM hoping that one of your entrepreneurial friends will magically refer you to a *unicorn*...  aka a talented copywriter with immediate availability and won’t force you to take out a reverse mortgage.



… the kind, that after realizing you can’t hire out, forces you to push back your launch date by 8 weeks while you rack up 361 star points at the local Starbucks and get to know every barista by name. 



Not a fan of Starbucks rewards? Don’t miss this workshop, damnit.



-Jo



PS. Is this a *CAN’T MISS*? 



Honestly… I have no clue. 



You tell me.



I mean… I wouldn’t offer you an extra ticket to a Radiohead show unless I knew for certain that you enjoyed listless walks in the rain and waxing philosophical over existentialism and other topics that make the average person’s head spin.



Same goes here.



We’re gonna pack this 60 minute show with an epic set list. 



We’ll play crowd pleasers like:



How copywriting has changed over the last few years, and how to take full advantage of those recent shifts in your next launch. (hint: as your software gets smarter, so must you) 



The *sneaky* 4-part email sequence that took under 2 hours to write and brought in an extra 150K+ in revenue on a client’s webinar launch.



Why most people get their closing sequence all wrong (OMG 12 hours left!!), and what to do instead in the final minutes of your launch to ethically and effortlessly attract more customers. 



But since our *intimate concert hall* aka GoToWebinar maxes out at 999 happy souls (I always save a seat for momma)... 



You should only make it to the show if…



You’re an online entrepreneur, coach, consultant, expert or influencer who already has a product you’re muchos proud of (or will in the near future) and want to be able to 2X, 3X or even 5X sales with powerful and easy-to-implement launch copy. 



	This be me. I’m in. 



You’re an established email or launch copywriter who charges the big bucks to do this for the people above -- and want to charge the even bigger bucks by getting better results in less time. 



	Dayum. It’s like you read my mind. 



You won’t miss racking up the Starbucks Points knowing full well you’ll be able to afford your own venti 5-shot flat white thank-you-vurry-much.



Still here? Punch your ticket.



Themeplate 2 - “The Bieber” 



About: 



“That’s all I needed you to say”.



Apologies can be powerful. Think of a situation where a couple has been fighting, and finally one of them puts aside their pride, and sincerely acknowledges (in full detail) their wrongdoings and apologizes. 



This:

Creates resourcefulness and renewed optimism 

Absolves the other person of any feelings or doubts that they were the one at fault. 



That’s what we’re activating here. 



Clearing the past so that our prospect can get more present and excited about where we’re taking them.



As a side note - when you apologize on behalf of other things/people/paradigms/oppressors in your industry, it subtly (but powerfully) positions you as a trusted (and benevolent) authority. 



THEMEPLATE:



[Apologize]

[Moment of Highest Tension]

[Other ways they’ve tried to solve it]

[Show that it wasn’t their fault]

[Offer an out - a better way] 

[The “altruistic CTA] 

[Qualify them in an exclusively-empowered way] 

[CTA] 

[PS: Sell the event with bullets]





EXAMPLE



Subject:

Sorry



Body:



I’m sorry, Ryan.



I’m sorry… 



… that the online course you just paid 5K for didn’t warn you about how hard it would be to execute all those tiny “email” icons on your funnel map.



… that the success of your funnel (and heck, your entire business) fully hinges on writing those little *things* and writing them fast.



… that an expertly-curated concoction of binaural beats, bulletproof coffee and brain drugs with bad names haven’t been enough to help you blast through it. 



And… 



… I’m sorry that your despite your best efforts to DIY, you’ve been struck down by confusing, over-complicated and impractical systems written by old (mostly dead) copywriters who didn’t have to contend with unceasing slack notifications, impossible deadlines, and even more impossible screaming 6 year olds.



So I’m really… reallllllllly sorry. 



And on behalf of all these things that have put you in this perilous place with the success of your launch dangling in the balance… 



… please accept my special invitation to make it up to you.



Starting next week, Jo & I will be hosting the Launch & Profit Workshops.



This is a *must attend* if you’re:



An online entrepreneur, coach, consultant, expert or influencer who already has a product you’re muchos proud of (or will in the vurrry near future) and want to be able to 2X, 3X or even 5X sales with powerful and easy-to-implement launch copy. 



A talented copywriter who charges the big-ish bucks to do this for the people above -- and wants to charge the even bigger bucks by getting better results in less time. 



The party gets started next Tuesday.



Click here to stay within blast radius for when the copy-bombs start falling.



(sign off)



PS. Here’s a small slice of how we’re gonna solve your “launch woes” once and for all…



Learn…



How copywriting has changed over the last few years, and how to take full advantage of those recent shifts in your next launch. (hint: as your software gets smarter, so do you) 



The *sneaky* 3-part email sequence that took 45 minutes to write and brought in an extra 154K in revenue on our last launch.



Why most people get their closing sequence all wrong (OMG 12 hours left!!), and what to do instead in the final minutes of your product launch. 



Don’t forget to grab your seat



Themeplate 3 - “The Way-Too-Early Ultimatum” 



About: 



Sometimes the BEST way to get a commitment, is for the prospect to see that they’re NOT committed to the opposite.



So by leading with a counter-intuitive invitation to exclude themselves, we get prospects to realize that they’re in fact committed to the result - and your pre-launch that delivers it. 



Send this as the third or FINAL invite into your webinar or launch funnel.



THEMEPLATE 



[CTA with urgency]

[Big outcome the launch is promising]

[Invitation to Opt-Out] 

[Frame inaction (of the opt out) as implied belongingness] 

[CTA 2]

[Exclusive Empowerment]

[CTA] 

[Optional PS to sell the event/launch/video]

[CTA] 



EXAMPLE: 



Subject: 

should I cross you off the list? 



Body:



Hey,



We’re kicking things off tomorrow with the first installment of our Profit & Launch Workshop series.



Just wanted to warn you that I’m gonna be in your inbox.



Like. 



A lot.



Mostly talking about online courses, product launches, evergreen funnels…



…  and how to write the kinda killer copy that amplifies the profits in all of’em.



If that’s not something you can get behind...



(For reals!? Why are you even here?) ← besides you, mom. 



Then kindly hit this link so I know NOT to hit you with the good stuff.  



*patiently waits for the room to clear*



…



…



WHAT!? 



Ryan!? 



You’re still here?



And you haven’t signed up for the VIP PASS that will get you live updates from the workshop series -- along with exclusive bonuses, perks, and priority access to the grand reveal next week?



Pshhh…



I’ll assume you accidentally deleted my last few emails in a fruitless attempt to hit Inbox Zero 



(there’s no such thing).



But there is such thing as expertly crafted email copy that adds EXTRA zeroes to your SamCart or Paypal dashboard. 



Especially if you’re an established coach, course creator, influencer or expert who’s in “funnel mode”...



… or a god-send of a copywriter who does this high-level stuff for the peeps mentioned above (and wants to get rewarded handsomely for it). 



Wanna see what I mean?



Click here to join the party.



(sign off)



PS. 



Tomorrow, we’ll be kicking things off with a bang. 



Revealing brand new material that hasn’t been shared anywhere else before.



The kinda copy wisdom that until now, has been reserved only for our high-level 1-on-1 clients (and the seldom drunk dial). 



Stuff like…



How copywriting has changed over the last few years, and how to take full advantage of those recent shifts in your next launch. (hint: as your software gets smarter, so must you) 



The *sneaky* 4-part email sequence that took less than 2 hours to write and brought in an extra 150K+ in revenue on a client’s webinar launch. 



Why most people get their closing sequence all wrong (OMG 12 hours left!!), and what to do instead in the final minutes of your product launch to lead more prospects across the finish line. 















Themeplate 4 - The “What this says about you” Confirmation (MVF2)



About: Most marketers totally fail to recognize the power of the confirmation email.



It’s the VERY first thing I look at when working with new clients, and generally speaking, their current version is some spin of the following:



“Thanks for signing up, you’re in, we’re gonna have a lot of fun, awesome, k bye”.



No additional value given. No confirmation of their decision. No adding meaning to their action. No teasing of what’s to come. 



And a whole stack of missed opportunities.



Time for a little upgrade.



General Format:



This one is less dependent on the structure - and more dependent on having the right ingredients.



What you want to be “selling” here are the 5C’s:



Confidence and reassurance in their decision

Confirm what this says about them (add meaning to the action). 

Clarity around the outcomes they can expect. 

Curiosity around the WAY the outcome will be delivered or experienced. 

Credibility that you can deliver it. 



THEMEPLATE



[Congratulate them on the previous action] 



[Confirm to them that this is a bigger deal than they may realize] 



[Diagnose what that action tells you about them - these are positive traits that you get to remind them of (and urge them to remain consistent with) later on when you make an offer]



[Tell them that they’ll be rewarded for that action]



[What pains/fears/frustrations they’ll soon no longer have to deal with - Moment of Highest Tension] 



[What pleasures/desires they’ll soon experience as their new normal] 



[Credibility bomb of why they can trust you to deliver it]



[Create a sense of ease that pre-supposes they’ll be doing something later Ie. “Nothing else to do now, but sit back, relax and get ready to (solve problem)] 



[SIgn off]



EXAMPLE:



Subject:

Thanks for confirming. Here’s what that says about you…



Body:



Hey <First Name>, 



On behalf of MILLIONS of teens across the country, I wanted to take a quick second to thank you for signing up for your free Video Series.



The first video should be landing in your inbox in just a few minutes. 



But first.



Some real talk. 



As the parent of a teen, I know things can be crazy tough on you sometimes.  



You want the best for your kid.



Your heart’s in the right place.



Yet despite your sincere, heartfelt efforts to lead them down a healthy, productive path, you keep smacking up against a wall of resistance. 



Acting out, aggressive outbursts, and stone-cold silence seems to be the “reward” for your care and concern. 



And if you’re feeling unappreciated, fearful, angry, impatient, and even downright resigned or hopeless…



All I can tell you is that this is normal.



You’re NOT alone.



And if you stick around, you’ll soon know how to expertly navigate your way through it. 



Here’s why I’m hopeful for you, <first name>. 



Despite all the challenges in front of you, you still FOUND this page and had the courage to put your name and email in that tiny box . 



This signals 3 things to me: 



You Still Abso-Freaking-Lutely Care. 



You’re NOT ready to give up.



You’re ready to empower yourself with the right tools and techniques to meet the challenges of the modern day parent. 



I’m proud to have you in the family.



And I can’t wait to reward your perseverance with the tools that will totally TRANSFORM your relationship with a teen in your life. 



Much much more to come. 



But for now, keep your eyes peeled for Video #1.



Catch you soon,



(sign off) 



P.S. If you’ve experienced any form of acting out or teenage rebelling over the last few months, the 6 minute video I’m about to send your way is an absolute MUST WATCH. 



P.P.S. Have you shared the love yet? 



The quickest way to relieve the pressure is to SHARE the responsibility.



The more parents, teachers and adults we can empower with these tools, the less it all falls squarely on our shoulders.





Themeplate 5 - The “What This Says About You” Webinar Confirmation



About: 



This is a variation of the themeplate above, except for a webinar registration. 



Even though someone registered for your webinar, it is NOT a given that they’re going to show up. 



And while it varies across industry, you’d be lucky if even 25% of your sign ups show up live.



Therefore, it’s kinda crazy that most marketers are willing to invest ad dollars in getting sign ups to their webinar only to let their system spit out automated replies until the day of the presentation.



This themplate is all about 2 things:



Selling the “show up”

Pre-Framing the sale. 



Like its earlier version, it boils down to 5C’s:



Confidence and reassurance in their decision

Confirm what it says about them (add meaning to the action)

Clarity around the outcomes they can expect. 

Curiosity around the WAY the outcome will be delivered. 

Credibility that you can deliver it. 



THEMEPLATE:



[Congratulate them on signing up for the webinar]



[Confirm to them that this is a bigger deal than they may have initially realized - especially important since most people aren’t too emotionally invested in a webinar sign up]



[Diagnose what this tells you about them]

These are positive traits that you get to remind them of (and urge them to remain consistent with) later on when you make an offer. 



[Remind them again that this is a big deal]



[State WHY it’s a big deal - tying it to your big promise or sales argument] 



[Tell them that they’ll be rewarded for that action]



[Remind them of the big promise - result they’ll get when they attend]



[What pains/fears/frustrations they’ll soon no longer have to deal with] 



[What pleasures/desires they’ll soon experience as their new normal] 



[Credibility bomb of why they can trust you to deliver it]



[SIgn off]



[P.S. Remind them of the webinar date/time] 



EXAMPLE: 



Subject: 

BOOM! You’re in. Here’s what that says about you…



Body:



You’re in, <first name>!



And i don’t say that lightly.



For in a single click, you just made the bold, irrevocable decision that:



Your company’s profits (and growth) will never be held hostage by bad copy (or overbooked + overpriced copywriters) again. 



Every marketing strategy you ever roll out (Paids Ads, Webinars, Content, etc.) will always be supported and amplified by high-converting email copy. 



And if you’re a copywriter…



You’ll be armed with the ROI-boosting email skillz that guarantee you’re never underbooked, never overpriced (no matter how much you raise your rates)... and always first in line when a new, A-list clients need a job done. 



It’s a pretty big deal.



Cause here’s the truth… 



Email copywriting is the single fastest (and least expensive) way of growing your profits WITHOUT drastically overhauling your existing strategy. 



And by saving your spot for this webinar…



You just put yourself first in line to learn the high impact email strategies that until now have been exclusively reserved for private clients like Neil Patel, Amy Porterfield, Intuit, Invision and Wistia just to name a few.



You’re now part of the fam.



And as such, you’ve earned your right to inherit the wealth. 



Nothing else to do for now, but lock it in your calendar and print out the cheat sheet to make sure that all the strategies you’ll be learning on Tuesday get put into action immediately. 



See you Tuesday,



Jo & Ry



PS: Once again, here are your webinar details:

DATE



TIME



LINK



PPS. 



I used to have this terrible habit of “collecting” webinars.



You know… signing up for them “just in case”, without any actual intention of showing up.



So I know it’s a “thing”. 



And I know there are some amongst us. 



I ain’t naming names. Most of you are solid AF…



But if you’re a “just-in-caser” consider this your lucky day. 



This *is* that one “case” that you’ll be thanking yourself later for. 



Because what you’ll learn in these 90 minutes can quite literally impact every aspect of your business moving forward. 



We cool?



Cool ;) 

Segmentation & Confirmation Checklist

Have I coached the prospect on:



Why they’re in a better position to achieve the outcome then anyone else?



Pride in previous accomplishments/circumstances?



Self-Selection (have i made self-selecting desirable and frictionless)? 



Meaning behind their click? 



A frictionless commitment to achieving the new outcome? 



Intimate awareness of their “Moment of Highest Tension” 



Excitement and renewed optimism around the outcome
